text: 2. Regarding to the deformation twinning, the type III grains with orientation ${ < } 1 1 1 { > } / / \mathrm { T A }$ direction are more inclined to develop double orientation twins. But few deformed twinning is activated in type I grains with orientation ${ < } 0 0 1 { > } / / \mathrm { T A }$ and the type II grains with orientation far from type III and I are more susceptible to the formation of single orientation twins. Regarding to the dislocation substructure, the type III grains are characterized by a tendency to form double dislocation glide traces. The type I grains are distinguished by the formation of typical dislocation cells. The type II grains exhibit a preference for the development of single dislocation glide traces.

3. The working hardening behaviors and strengthening contribution are strongly affected by the microstructure evolution at both RT and LNT. Multiple strain hardening mechanisms of the lightweight cryogenic steel were observed at LNT. It is revealed that an almost constant hardening rate was obtained in the early stage of deformation due to the primary deformation twinning. Then a continuous decrease of the hardening rate resulting from the heavily activated cross slip. For high strain, a constant work hardening is reached again due to the extensive activation of primary twinning and secondary twinning, which in turn facilitates multi-stage strain hardening. During deformation at room temperature, dislocation hardening but not the deformation twinning is the principal contributor to strain hardening. ![](images/70819dd812e89bb9fc786c10f413276ec24996bf7f20b0200b259496028b73d2.jpg) <details> <summary>bar_line</summary> (a) | True strain | Measured True Stress (MPa) | σ_dis (MPa) | σ_GB (MPa) | σ_f (MPa) | | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | | 0.0 | 250 | 100 | 150 | 150 | | 0.1 | 480 | 250 | 150 | 150 | | 0.2 | 620 | 350 | 150 | 150 | | 0.3 | 720 | 450 | 150 | 150 | | 0.4 | 800 | 0 | 0 | 0 | </details> ![](images/df06c6c1be6d022b8aa0dd40f25bdcce20a633bb4545c7caf4517e8e403f55d4.jpg) <details> <summary>bar_stacked</summary> | True strain | Measured True Stress (MPa) | σ_twin (MPa) | σ_dis (MPa) | σ_GB (MPa) | σ_f (MPa) | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | 0.0 | 500 | 100 | 100 | 100 | 400 | | 0.1 | 780 | 200 | 300 | 150 | 400 | | 0.2 | 1000 | 300 | 450 | 200 | 400 | | 0.3 | 1280 | 400 | 550 | 250 | 400 | | 0.4 | 1450 | 550 | 650 | 300 | 400 | </details> Fig. 13. Contributions of various strengthening mechanisms to flow stress at different temperatures: (a) Room temperature (RT). (b) Liquid nitrogen temperature (LNT).

4. An excellent combination of the strength, ductility and impact toughness is obtained in the designed low-carbon low-density steel. It is shown that yield strength, tensile strength, ductility and impact tough are 251 MPa, 580 MPa, 50 % and 331J at RT, and 497 MPa, 945 MPa, 75.5 % and 254J at LNT, which is superior than that not only the conventional 9Ni-steel due to both excellent mechanical properties but the low-cost, but better than that of the conventional austenitic steel due to it low-density. Thes advantages make the designed low-carbon low-density steel of Fe–35Mn–6Al–0.1C (wt.%) to be one of the promising candidates of the cryogenic steel in the future.

5. The high stacking fault energy (SFE) twinning and its orientation dependence are mainly attributed to the stress concentration induced by planar dislocation substructure. Type II and III grains form cell block walls with planar slip characteristics, which led to stress concentration required to activate twinning. Type I grains formed uniform dislocation cells, which are not conducive to the formation of high stress required to activate twinning. The interaction between dislocation and SRO may lower the critical twinning stress and promote planar slip of dislocations. # CRediT authorship contribution statement Qingfeng Kang: Writing – original draft, Methodology, Formal analysis, Data curation. Zexi Zhang: Validation, Methodology, Investigation. Ziyuan Gao: Supervision, Methodology, Investigation. Cunyu Wang: Software, Resources, Data curation. Jianxiong Liang: Software, Resources, Project administration. Zhengdong Liu: Validation, Resources, Methodology. Yuqing Weng: Resources, Methodology, Conceptualization. Wenquan Cao: Writing – review & editing, Supervision, Resources, Methodology, Funding acquisition, Conceptualization. # Declaration of competing interest The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper. # Acknowledgement This research is supported by the Natural Nature Science Foundation of China with granted item number of 51871062 and 51371052. # Data availability Data will be made available on request.
